calendar.matters:
Petition For Final Discharge
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

There being no appearance by Andrew Dimitriou, the Court ordered all matters off calendar noting that if Mr. Dimitriou wished he could apply again.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Off Calendar

Petition For Final Discharge
Off Calendar Jun-26-2013 Continued To Aug-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

A further Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Andrew Dimitriou to appear on August 13, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dpet. 204. Sanctions are increased to $30.00 per day.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jun-26-2013 Continued To Aug-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Final Discharge
Off Calendar May-15-2013 Continued To Jun-26-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Mr. Dimitriou was to do an accounting of the funds. This matter has been on the Court's calendar four times. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Andrew Dimitriou to appear on June 26, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the accounting has not been filed. If no appearance is made on that date, sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will begin to run. The status hearing was also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Final Discharge
Off Calendar Apr-03-2013 Continued To May-15-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Demetrios Dimitriou (Executor)

Mr. Dimitriou reported that he received the final receipt from SPCA and waiver of accounting yesterday, that he is missing three bank statements, and requested a one month continuance. The Court advised Mr. Dimitriou that just an accounting on the reserve was needed. Mr. Dimitriou explained that there was an outstanding tax bill and that he will provide a copy of the tax bill and receipts. The Petition for Final Discharge was continued to May 15,2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy Loos.

Petition For Final Discharge
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Continued To Apr-03-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Final Discharge
Off Calendar Mar-05-2013 Continued To Mar-19-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Demetrios Dimitrou.

Mr. Dimitrou reported that there was a mathmatical error of $167.75 and the he has sent a check to the SPCA. Furthermore, he is awaiting waiver of accountings from the SPCA and the Salvation Army and that he had requested a refund from the Salvation Army. The matter was continued to March 19, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Ella Yip/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jul-19-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for and with Demetrios Dimitriou (Executor).

Mr. Dimitriou stated that there was no fee sharing between his firm to the Executor or from the Exector to his firm. In addition, he stated that he was not able to speak to Ms. Cuddy, that he spoke to her Children only, that the Daughter told him that the Children wanted the money to go to the Mom and that he spoke to the Son who confirmed it. Furthermore, he stated that he asked for disclaimers or 13100 Declarations, that the Children said they would not open probate for $5,000.00, that he could not force them to provide him with the disclaimers or the 13100 Declarations, that he did not inquire whether John Cuddy died with a will, that he just asked who the Beneficiaries were and that he suggested they seek Counsel. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Application For Letters Of Special Admnistration
Off Calendar Dec-02-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Application For Letters Of Special Admnistration
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Continued To Dec-02-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 202

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-02-2009 Continued To Dec-14-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Demetrios Dimitriou.

The Court stated that there was an issue with notice to the heirs. Mr. Dimitriou stated he was confused because the notes stated that he needed to list the ancestors. The Court stated that there needs to be more information and that notice is to be sent to the heirs at law. Mr. Dimitriou stated that he doesn't have the date of death of the parents, that he has a telephone number for a Cousin (Sally) on the Mother's side in the U.S. but the number doesn't work, that he sent notice to Flint, Michigan, that the notice was returned, that there are three Cousin's on the Father's side in England, and that none of their notices came back. In addition, he stated that he did receive a call from one of the Cousins (Ann Mitchell) in England inquiring as to how Ms. Crocker passed away, that he did get her phone number but he must have transposed numbers, and that since he does not have a good phone number for her he could overnight an inquiry to her. He also stated that he had sent all of them a notice of probate. The Court directed Mr. Dimitriou to write to the four individuals to obtain the name of their parents, if their parents aren't alive the Court wants the dates of their deaths, Counsel is to inquire about the names of any other Cousins, and is to obtain their e-mail addresses and telephone numbers. The Court noted that there was a Postal Code on the notice sent to Elisabeth Rickard and informed Mr. Dimitriou that it looked like there was an omission of the Postal Code on the notice to Marguerite Williams. The Court then continued the matter to December 14, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy McConico.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-19-2009 Continued To Nov-02-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
